name: Triple Goddess
shortDescription: A modern Neopagan concept of a deity who exists as three distinct aspects or figures, often Maiden, Mother, and Crone, representing different stages of life and aspects of divinity.
description: The Triple Goddess is a Neopagan and Wiccan concept of a deity revered in three distinct forms or aspects, often represented as the Maiden, Mother, and Crone. This triplicity reflects the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth, and embodies different stages of womanhood and aspects of divinity. The Maiden represents youth, purity, new beginnings, and enchantment. The Mother embodies maturity, fertility, nurturing, and creation. The Crone signifies wisdom, endings, transformation, and the mysteries of death. While not explicitly present in pre-Christian Celtic mythology in the exact form it is understood today, the concept draws inspiration from various Celtic goddesses who exhibit multiple aspects or associations. For example, goddesses like Brigid, associated with fire, poetry, and healing, and the Morrigan, a war goddess with shapeshifting abilities, can be interpreted as embodying aspects of the Triple Goddess archetype. The Triple Goddess is a central figure in many Wiccan and Neopagan traditions, symbolizing the interconnectedness of all things and the power of the feminine divine. The concept resonates with similar trinities found in other mythologies and religions around the world, and serves as a powerful symbol of female empowerment and the cyclical nature of existence. The symbol is often associated with the moon cycles and the phases of the moon which are a visual representation of the three aspects.

sources.title: Triumph of the Moon: A History of Modern Pagan Witchcraft
sources.author: Ronald Hutton

sources.title: The Witches' God: Lord of the Dance
sources.author: Janet and Stewart Farrar
